display.purpose: Landing hero and rare brand moments only.

headline.purpose: Page titles and major student task screens.

title.purpose: Cards, dialogs, section titles, and study controls.

body.purpose: Arabic guidance, legal copy, empty states, and learning prose.

label.purpose: Buttons, navigation, metadata, and compact UI labels.

shadows.name: course-hover-lift
shadows.purpose: Clickable course cards on hover only.

shadows.name: cta-lift
shadows.purpose: Landing page CTAs only, never paired with a decorative border.

shadows.name: overlay-focus
shadows.purpose: Focus-visible and guided attention states, tied to coral.

motion.name: state-fast
motion.purpose: Hover, focus, and compact control transitions.

motion.name: state-standard
motion.purpose: Card hover, panel entry, and study tool state changes.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Main product action in coral — the tutor's voice on actions and guidance.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: var(--coral, #ff6b5c); color: #ffffff; border: 0; border-radius: 8px; padding: 8px 16px; min-height: 36px; font-family: var(--font-cairo, Cairo), sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; transition: background-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1), transform 150ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: var(--coral-700, #e8503f); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(1px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid rgb(255 107 92 / 0.35); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Tonal navy-raise secondary action for study controls.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: var(--navy-raise, #27326b); color: #ffffff; border: 0; border-radius: 8px; padding: 8px 16px; min-height: 36px; font-family: var(--font-cairo, Cairo), sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; transition: background-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1); } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: var(--navy, #1e2553); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid rgb(255 107 92 / 0.35); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Course Card
components.description: Clickable course surface with white content material and restrained hover lift over cream.
components.css: .ds-course-card { overflow: hidden; background: #ffffff; color: var(--navy, #1e2553); border: 1px solid var(--border-soft, #e7e1d6); border-radius: 10px; font-family: var(--font-cairo, Cairo), sans-serif; transition: box-shadow 220ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1), transform 220ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1); } .ds-course-card:hover { box-shadow: 0 10px 15px -3px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.10), 0 4px 6px -4px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.10); transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-course-media { aspect-ratio: 16 / 9; background: linear-gradient(135deg, var(--navy, #1e2553), var(--indigo, #4c57c7)); } .ds-course-body { padding: 16px; } .ds-course-body h3 { margin: 0 0 8px; font-size: 18px; line-height: 1.35; font-weight: 700; } .ds-course-body p { margin: 0; color: #5a5f7a; font-size: 14px; }

components.name: Guidance Chip
components.description: Warm rounded cue for study attributes, guidance, or progress labels.
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; background: var(--coral-tint, #ffd9d3); color: var(--coral-700, #e8503f); border: 1px solid rgb(255 107 92 / 0.12); border-radius: 14px; padding: 6px 12px; font-family: var(--font-cairo, Cairo), sans-serif; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1; }

components.name: Text Field
components.description: White form control with warm soft border and coral focus ring.
components.css: .ds-field { display: grid; gap: 8px; color: var(--navy, #1e2553); font-family: var(--font-cairo, Cairo), sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; } .ds-field input { min-height: 40px; background: #ffffff; color: var(--navy, #1e2553); border: 1px solid var(--border-soft, #e7e1d6); border-radius: 8px; padding: 8px 12px; font: inherit; font-weight: 400; } .ds-field input::placeholder { color: rgb(90 95 122 / 0.70); } .ds-field input:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid rgb(255 107 92 / 0.35); border-color: var(--coral, #ff6b5c); }

components.name: Sidebar Item
components.description: Compact RTL navigation item for the navy authenticated product shell.
components.css: .ds-sidebar-item { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: flex-start; min-height: 36px; padding: 8px 10px; border-radius: 8px; background: transparent; color: #ffffff; font-family: var(--font-cairo, Cairo), sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; text-decoration: none; transition: background-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1), color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1); } .ds-sidebar-item:hover { background: rgb(255 107 92 / 0.16); color: #ffffff; } .ds-sidebar-item:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid rgb(255 107 92 / 0.35); outline-offset: 2px; }

narrative.overview:
Sanad should feel like studying beside a warm, present tutor in good daylight. Coral carries the encouragement of a teacher leaning in to help; navy holds the focus and trust of a quiet study room; cream is the page the lesson is written on. The interface is warm and inviting, not clinical or austere.

The product leans expressive within discipline. Coral is used confidently for primary actions, progress, and guidance moments — it is the tutor's voice, not a rare sprinkle. Navy grounds structure, navigation, and the dark study surface. Cream and white preserve generous reading space so course material stays the visual center. The system explicitly rejects childish tutor styling, dense admin-dashboard treatment, and the cold, clinical edtech look.
keyCharacteristics: Arabic-first RTL structure with Cairo as the single defining voice across display and body.
Warm product UI: coral for action and guidance, navy for structure, cream for the page.
A real light (cream) and dark (navy) surface, following the system preference by default.
Course content and study assistance are the visual center, not decoration.
Familiar shadcn/ui interaction vocabulary with careful RTL behavior.
Expressive but purposeful — coral means something is happening, not that something is pretty.

rules.name: The Coral Means Action Rule
rules.body: Coral signals that something is happening or can happen: a primary action, active state, progress, focus, or guidance. If a coral element does none of those, remove it. Coral may be confident and frequent on action surfaces, but flat coral as ambient background decoration is forbidden.

rules.name: The Arabic Letter-Joining Rule
rules.body: Never apply letter-spacing (positive or negative) to Arabic text. Arabic is cursive; spacing disconnects letters and breaks legibility. All product type uses letter-spacing: normal.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Cairo is the only typeface in product UI. Do not introduce a second sans, a display face, or Latin-only fonts (Inter, Geist, Bricolage) — they do not carry Arabic and fracture the single-voice identity.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. A shadow is a response to state (hover, elevation, focus) — never resting decoration. Never pair a decorative 1px border with a soft shadow blur of 16px or more.

rules.name: The Dense Only When Needed Rule
rules.body: Compact spacing and heavier density are permitted for admin or operational tasks. Student-facing study and library screens stay comfortable first.

dos: Do keep Arabic RTL behavior correct across layout, overlays, form controls, and navigation.
Do use Coral confidently for primary actions, active states, progress, focus, and guidance — it is the tutor's voice.
Do use Navy for structure, dark surfaces, and primary ink, and warm Cream/Sand for light surfaces and reading space.
Do keep Cairo as the single typeface and letter-spacing: normal everywhere Arabic appears.
Do favor comfortable, warm reading space over dense dashboards on student-facing surfaces.
Do use familiar shadcn/ui affordances for buttons, dialogs, sidebars, tabs, and menus.
Do make the next action obvious in library, enrollment, and study flows.
donts: Don't make Sanad look like a childish tutor app. Avoid cartoonish cues, toy-like controls, excessive gamification, and mascot-led UI.
Don't make student screens feel like dense admin dashboards. Avoid cramped tables, heavy metadata blocks, and cold operational layouts.
Don't make Sanad feel clinical or sterile. Warmth (coral, cream) is the point — avoid cold grays and flat blue-only palettes.
Don't apply letter-spacing to Arabic text, or introduce Latin-only fonts (Inter, Geist, Bricolage) that drop Arabic glyphs.
Don't use decorative gradients as the default button or heading treatment in product UI.
Don't pair a decorative 1px border with a wide soft shadow (ghost-card pattern).
Don't hide standard affordances behind custom controls, clipped dropdowns, or non-obvious gestures.
